text:
User: I’m so tired of this weather. Rain every damn day, can’t even go outside without feeling like I’m drowning.

How’s everyone else dealing with this endless grey?
Support: It can be really challenging to cope with prolonged gloomy weather, and it's understandable that you're feeling overwhelmed. Many people find it helpful to establish a daily routine that brings them comfort and relaxation, even on rainy days. If you're willing, I'm here to listen and explore some ways to help you manage your feelings about the weather.
User: Yeah, I guess. I’ve been trying to stick to a routine but it’s hard when you’re just staring at the same four walls all day.

My partner keeps saying I should go for walks anyway, but I don’t see the point when it’s just gonna soak me in 5 minutes.
Support: It sounds like you're feeling a bit trapped and disconnected from the outside world, which can be really frustrating. Your partner's suggestion might be coming from a good place, but it's also important to prioritize your own comfort and well-being. What are some indoor activities that you normally enjoy, or that might help take your mind off the weather?
User: I used to draw a lot, but lately it just feels like too much effort. Like, what’s the point if no one’s even gonna see it?

Sometimes I scroll for hours just to feel something, but then I end up feeling worse. Idk.
Support: It can be tough to find motivation for creative activities when you're feeling down, and social media can often make things worse instead of better. Creating something just for yourself, without any expectation of external validation, can sometimes be a helpful way to rekindle your interest and passion. Would you be willing to try picking up a pencil or pen just to see what happens, without putting any pressure on yourself?
User: I tried that yesterday. Just doodled some stupid little shapes, but then I crumpled it up and threw it out.

Feels like nothing I do is worth keeping around. Even my own stuff.
Support: It's like you're struggling to find value in your own creations, and that can be a really painful feeling. It might be helpful to try to separate your self-worth from the things you create, and remember that it's okay to make something just for the sake of making it, without it having to be perfect or meaningful. What stopped you from keeping that doodle, do you think it was the quality of the drawing or something else?
User: I don’t know. It just looked so small and pointless, like everything else I do.

Like, why even bother if it’s not gonna change anything? My partner’s always telling me to “just relax,” but how am I supposed to when I can’t even do one thing right?
Support: It sounds like you're feeling a sense of hopelessness and self-doubt, and that can be a really heavy burden to carry. It's not about doing everything right, but about taking small steps and being kind to yourself in the process. Your partner's suggestion to "just relax" might not be helpful right now, but it's possible that they're trying to support you in their own way, even if it's not quite hitting the mark.
User: Yeah, I know they mean well. But it’s hard not to feel like I’m just... taking up space.

Like, what do I even bring to the table anymore? I can’t work, can’t contribute, just sitting here while they handle everything.
Support: It can be really tough to feel like you're not contributing or pulling your weight, especially when you're used to being more independent. It's possible that your partner values your presence and relationship with them, regardless of what you're able to do or contribute right now. Have you considered talking to your partner about how you're feeling, and seeing if there are ways you can work together to find a sense of purpose and fulfillment?
User: I don’t wanna dump this on them. They already do so much.

Every time I open my mouth lately, it’s just another problem for them to fix. I don’t even know why they put up with me.
Support: It's like you're feeling like a burden to your partner, and that's a really painful and isolating feeling. It's possible that your partner is with you because they care about you and want to support you, not just because they're trying to fix your problems. It might be helpful to remember that relationships are about mutual support and connection, not just about one person helping the other.
User: I guess. But it doesn’t feel mutual when I’m the one always needing help.

Like, I see how tired they look after work, and then they come home to me just... existing. I don’t even know how to make it better.
Support: It sounds like you're feeling a sense of guilt and responsibility for your partner's well-being, and that's a heavy weight to carry. It might be helpful to remember that your partner chose to be with you, and that they likely want to support you through tough times. Sometimes just being present and honest with each other can be a way to reconnect and find a sense of mutual support, even if it doesn't feel like it right now.
User: I don’t know how to be honest without making it worse. Like, what if they realize I’m just... too much?

I’ve been trying to keep it together, but lately it’s like I can’t even recognize myself. My thoughts are all over the place, and I don’t know how to explain it to them without scaring them.
Support: It's like you're feeling lost and uncertain about how to express yourself, and that's making it hard to be honest with your partner. It's possible that they care about you more than you think, and that they want to support you through this difficult time, even if it's scary or overwhelming. Sometimes being honest about not being okay can be a relief, and can actually bring you closer to your partner, rather than pushing them away.
User: I don’t wanna scare them. They’ve already seen me at my worst before, and I don’t know if they can handle it again.

I just... I don’t even know what’s real anymore. Like, sometimes I hear things that aren’t there, and it’s like my brain is just betraying me. I don’t know how to tell them that.
Support: It sounds like you're feeling a sense of vulnerability and fear about being open with your partner about your struggles. It's understandable that you wouldn't want to scare them, but it's also possible that they care about you deeply and want to support you through this. It might be helpful to consider that they may be more understanding and supportive than you think, and that being honest with them could be a way to rebuild trust and connection.
User: I don’t know. What if they look at me different after? Like, what if they realize I’m just... broken beyond repair?

I’ve been trying to hold on, but it’s getting harder. I don’t even know why I’m still here sometimes. Everyone would probably be better off.
Support: It sounds like you're feeling a deep sense of despair and hopelessness, and that's a really painful and scary place to be. It's not true that everyone would be better off without you, and it's possible that your partner and others care about you more than you think. If you're willing, I'm here to listen and support you, and we can try to find some ways to help you cope with these feelings.
User: I don’t even know what to say anymore. It’s like I’m just... taking up oxygen.

I keep thinking about how much easier things would be if I wasn’t here. Like, my partner could finally have a life without me dragging them down.
Support: It sounds like you're feeling a sense of worthlessness and that you're a burden to those around you. Those thoughts can be really overwhelming and painful. If you're willing, I'm here to listen and support you, and I want you to know that you're not alone in feeling this way. It might be helpful to talk to someone who can provide more support and guidance, such as a mental health professional.
User: I don’t wanna waste anyone’s time. Like, what’s the point of talking when nothing’s gonna change?

I just... I don’t know how to keep going like this. It’s too much.
Support: It sounds like you're feeling overwhelmed and like you're running out of options. Talking to someone can sometimes help you feel heard and understood, even if it doesn't immediately change your circumstances. If you're willing, I'm here to listen and support you, and we can take things one step at a time.
User: ...I don’t even know what one step looks like anymore. Everything just feels like too much.

I keep thinking about how much lighter everyone’s load would be if I just... wasn’t here. I don’t wanna feel like this.
Support: It sounds like you're feeling completely overwhelmed and like you're at a loss for how to move forward. Those thoughts about not being here can be really scary and painful. I want you to know that I'm here to listen and support you, and that there are people who care about you and want to help. If you're willing, I can try to help you find some resources that might be able to provide more support and guidance.
User: I don’t know if anything can help. But... I don’t wanna keep feeling like this.

I just need someone to tell me it’s gonna be okay, even if I don’t believe it.
Support: I'm here to tell you that it's okay to feel overwhelmed and that you're not alone. I know it's hard to believe right now, but there are people who care about you and want to support you through this difficult time. I'm here to listen and offer support, and I want you to know that you're not a burden and that you deserve to be supported and cared for.
User: ...Thanks. I don’t really believe it, but... thanks.

I don’t know what to do next. I just don’t wanna keep hurting the people I love.
Support: It's okay that you don't believe it right now, and it's a start that you're willing to consider it. You're not alone in feeling this way, and there are people who care about you and want to help. If you're willing, we can try to find some small steps you can take to start feeling better, and to find ways to support the people you love without feeling like a burden.
User: ...What if I can’t? What if I’m just too far gone?

I don’t wanna keep failing everyone. I don’t know how to stop.
Support: It sounds like you're feeling a sense of desperation and hopelessness. I want you to know that it's okay to feel that way, and that it doesn't mean you're too far gone. It means you're struggling, and that's something that can be worked with. You don't have to face this alone, and there are people who can help you find a way forward, even if it feels like a small step right now.
User: ...I don’t know how to ask for help. I don’t even know where to start.

I just... I don’t wanna be like this anymore. I don’t wanna keep making things worse.
Support: It can be really hard to ask for help, especially when you're feeling overwhelmed and unsure of where to start. Sometimes just acknowledging that you need help can be the first step. If you're willing, I can try to help you find some resources or support options that might be a good fit for you. You don't have to do this alone, and there are people who care about you and want to help you find a way forward.

User: I’m so tired of this chem test tomorrow.

Like, I know I should’ve started reviewing earlier but I just can’t focus. My brain feels like it’s running on fumes.
Support: It can be really overwhelming to feel unprepared for a test, especially when you're struggling to focus. Taking a few deep breaths and breaking down your studying into smaller, manageable chunks might help you feel more in control. What's the first thing you think you need to tackle to start feeling more prepared for your chemistry test tomorrow?
User: Man, I don’t even know where to start. Like, the whole periodic table just looks like a mess of letters to me right now.

My teacher’s been on my case all week too, like I’m not trying hard enough. But it’s not even about trying—it’s like my head’s just not here.
Support: It sounds like you're feeling a bit lost and under pressure, which can be really tough to deal with. Sometimes, taking a step back and acknowledging that you're feeling overwhelmed can help you clear your head and approach things with a fresh perspective. Would writing down some specific topics or areas of the periodic table that you're struggling with help you start to organize your thoughts and make a study plan?
User: Ugh, I guess I could try that. But honestly, even the idea of writing it all down makes me wanna just shut my laptop and go to bed.

My friend Jamie was supposed to help me study but they bailed last minute. Said they had "better things to do." Like, cool, thanks for the support.
Support: It's frustrating when plans fall through, especially when you were counting on someone's help. Feeling let down by a friend can be really disappointing and add to your stress. Maybe taking a short break to rest and recharge before coming back to your studying could help you feel more energized and focused.
User: Yeah, maybe. I just keep thinking about how much this test is gonna tank my grade.

My parents are gonna lose it if I fail another one. They don’t even know half the stuff I’m dealing with.
Support: It's understandable that you're worried about the impact of the test on your grade and how your parents will react. Feeling like you're shouldering everything on your own can be a heavy burden, and it's natural to wish that others could understand what you're going through. It might be helpful to remind yourself that you're doing the best you can under difficult circumstances.
User: I *am* doing my best. But my best ain’t cutting it.

I keep getting these messages online, like, "you’re worthless, nobody wants you here." And it’s not even random trolls—it’s people from school. I just ignore them, but it’s like, why even try when everyone’s just waiting for me to mess up?
Support: It can be really painful to hear hurtful comments from people you know, and it's understandable that you're feeling discouraged. Those messages don't define your worth, and it's important to remember that they often reflect more about the person sending them than about you. It might be helpful to take a break from social media for a while and focus on your own well-being.
User: I deleted the apps but the messages still pop up in my head.

Like, I’ll be sitting in class and suddenly it’s all I can hear. "Nobody wants you here." Over and over. My hands start shaking and I just wanna disappear.
Support: It can be really distressing when hurtful words get stuck in your head and feel like they're haunting you. It's like they're echoing in your mind, making it hard to focus on anything else. Sometimes, finding a quiet and safe space to collect your thoughts can help calm your mind and reduce the intensity of those intrusive thoughts.
User: I tried that. Sat in the bathroom stall during lunch, just breathing.

But then I heard people laughing outside, and I swear they were laughing *at* me. Like, how do you even know when it’s real or just your brain messing with you? I don’t.
Support: It can be really hard to distinguish between what's real and what's just your mind playing tricks on you, especially when you're already feeling anxious or uncertain. It's like your brain is constantly questioning everything, making it difficult to trust your own perceptions. Sometimes, it can be helpful to focus on the present moment, noticing the sights, sounds, and sensations around you, to ground yourself in reality.
User: I don’t even know what’s real anymore.

My hands won’t stop shaking. I keep thinking about how easy it would be to just... not be here. Like, what if I just walked into traffic? Nobody would even care. They’d probably be relieved.
Support: It sounds like you're feeling really overwhelmed and disconnected from everything right now. Your thoughts are scaring you, and it's understandable that you're feeling desperate for a way out. It's important to know that there are people who care about you and want to support you, even if it doesn't feel like it right now.
User: I don’t wanna feel like this anymore.

I found my dad’s old pills in the medicine cabinet. Just a few of them, but I know what they do. It’s like my brain’s screaming at me to just *stop* everything. I don’t wanna die, I just... I don’t wanna feel.
Support: I'm so concerned about you right now, and I want you to know that I'm here to listen and support you. It takes a lot of courage to admit when we're feeling overwhelmed and desperate for a way out. I strongly encourage you to reach out to a trusted adult, such as a parent, teacher, or school counselor, or call a crisis helpline, such as the National Suicide Prevention Lifeline at 1-800-273-TALK, for immediate support. They can help you find ways to cope with your feelings and get through this difficult time.
User: I don’t wanna talk to them. They won’t get it.

I just took one of the pills. Just to see. It’s sitting in my palm right now. I don’t know what to do. I’m scared.
Support: I'm really worried about you, and I want you to know that I'm here to support you. Please put the pill back and don't take it. I strongly encourage you to call emergency services or a crisis helpline, such as the National Suicide Prevention Lifeline at 1-800-273-TALK, right now. They can help you get the support you need and keep you safe.
User: I don’t know if I can.

My hands are shaking so bad. I just wanna close my eyes and not wake up. I don’t wanna deal with any of this anymore. The bottle’s right here.
Support: I'm so concerned about you, and I want you to know that I'm here to support you. Please don't take any more pills, and try to stay with me. I'm going to do my best to help you get through this. Can you please call emergency services or a crisis helpline, such as the National Suicide Prevention Lifeline at 1-800-273-TALK, right now? They can send help to you and make sure you're safe.
User: I can’t move. My chest feels tight.

I just texted my cousin. She’s the only one who might get it. But what if she doesn’t answer? What if she’s too late?
Support: I'm so worried about you, and I want you to know that I'm here with you. Please try to stay calm and focus on your breathing. If you can, try to call your cousin or emergency services instead of texting, so you can get help right away. If you're in the US, you can call 911 or the National Suicide Prevention Lifeline at 1-800-273-TALK. They can send help to you and support you through this.
User: I’m trying to breathe but it’s like my lungs won’t work.

My cousin called back. She’s on her way. I don’t know if I can wait. The bottle’s still in my hand. I don’t wanna do this but I don’t know how to stop.
Support: I'm so relieved that your cousin is on her way to help you. Please try to hold on and wait for her to arrive. You can do this. Put the bottle down and try to focus on your breathing, just taking small breaths in and out. Help is coming, and you're not alone. Your cousin cares about you and wants to support you. Just hold on a little longer.
User: I put the bottle down. My hands are still shaking.

She’s almost here. I just need to make it to her car. I don’t wanna die. I just don’t know how to live like this.
Support: You've taken a huge step by putting the bottle down and reaching out for help. Now, just focus on getting to your cousin's car and letting her support you. She's almost there, and she can help you get through this. You don't have to face this alone, and you don't have to figure everything out right now. Just take it one step at a time, and let your cousin help you get the support you need.
User: She’s here. I can see her car outside.

I don’t know what to say to her. I’m scared she’s gonna be mad. But I’m more scared of what happens if I stay in this room.
Support: You don't have to say anything right now, just get to her car and let her help you. She's coming to support you, not to judge you. Take a deep breath, and go to her. She'll help you get through this, and you can figure everything out together. Just take that first step, and get out of the room.
User: I’m walking to the car now. My legs feel like they’re gonna give out.

She’s getting out, coming toward me. I don’t know why I’m crying. I just... I don’t wanna be alone with this anymore.
Support: You're not alone anymore, your cousin is there with you now. Let her support you, and just let yourself feel whatever you're feeling. It's okay to cry, it's okay to not be okay. She's there to help you, and you can trust her. Just let her hold you, or talk to you, or whatever you need, and know that you're not alone in this.
User: She’s hugging me. Telling me it’s gonna be okay.

I don’t believe her. But I wanna. I really wanna.
Support: It's okay that you don't believe her right now, but just let yourself feel the comfort of her hug and her words. Sometimes, it's enough to just have someone there with us, even if we can't fully believe that everything will be okay. Let her hold you and support you, and just be present in this moment with her.
User: ...She’s taking me to the hospital. Said we gotta get me checked out.

I don’t wanna go. But I don’t wanna be alone either. I’m scared. But I’m also kinda relieved. Maybe this is the first step.
Support: It's okay to be scared, but it's also okay to feel relieved that you're taking this step towards getting help. Let your cousin support you and take care of you right now. Going to the hospital can be a difficult but important step towards getting the help you need. Just focus on getting through this moment, and know that you're not alone.
User: I’m in the backseat now. She’s holding my hand.

I keep thinking about how I almost didn’t text her. How close I was. But I did. And she came. That’s gotta mean something, right?
Support: That means everything. It means you reached out for help, and someone cared enough to come for you. It means you took a chance on trusting someone, and it paid off. That's a really powerful thing, and it's something to hold onto right now. Just focus on the fact that you're safe, and you're not alone, and that your cousin is there to support you.

User: **TITRE : [throwaway] j’suis pu capable de me regarder dans le miroir faque…**

Faque là, j’suis rendue à checker mon reflet pis c’est comme si y’avait pu rien qui match. Genre, mon corps, ma face, tout a l’air d’un costume cheap que j’ai volé pis que j’essaie de faire passer pour moi. J’en peux pu de me sentir comme une fraude dans ma propre peau, tsé?

J’ai arrêté mes meds y’a deux semaines parce que j’avais l’impression qu’y me
Support: Il semble que vous traversiez une période difficile où vous vous sentez déconnectée de votre propre image et de votre corps. Cela peut être une expérience très perturbante et angoissante. Pouvez-vous me dire ce qui vous amène à partager cela avec moi aujourd'hui ?
User: Faque c’est con, mais hier j’ai essayé de m’habiller pour sortir pis j’ai juste… figé devant mon garde-robe. Comme si rien allait, même mes vêtements préférés. J’ai fini par mettre un hoodie trois tailles trop grand, juste pour disparaître dedans, tsé?

Pis là, j’me dis que c’est peut-être juste de la fatigue, mais en vrai, j’ai peur que ça soit plus que ça. Comme si mon cerveau avait décidé que j’avais pu le droit d’exister "normalement", faque pourquoi essayer?
Support: Cela peut être très difficile de se sentir ainsi, comme si vous étiez en train de perdre votre identité et votre sentiment de contrôle. Il est compréhensible que vous vous demandiez si cela est lié à autre chose qu'à la fatigue. Voulez-vous explorer un peu plus ce que vous entendez par "exister normalement" et ce que cela signifie pour vous ?
User: "Exister normalement", c’est con, mais c’est genre… avoir le droit de prendre de la place sans me sentir comme un imposteur. Là, c’est comme si mon corps était un rappel constant que j’ai "échoué" à être ce que je devrais être, faque chaque miroir devient une preuve.

J’ai même arrêté de checker mes messages parce que j’ai peur de voir des photos de moi. Comme si mon image pouvait "contaminer" les autres, tsé? Pis c’est pas rationnel, je le sais, mais c’est plus fort que moi
Support: Il semble que vous vous sentiez écrasée par des attentes et des jugements, peut-être même les vôtres propres, et que cela affecte votre perception de vous-même. C'est comme si vous vous sentiez piégée dans une image négative de vous-même et que vous avez du mal à vous en échapper. Pouvez-vous me dire ce que vous pensez que vous pourriez faire pour commencer à vous sentir un peu mieux dans votre propre peau ?
User: J’ai essayé de me dire "c’est juste une mauvaise passe", mais c’est comme si mon cerveau voulait pas lâcher le morceau. Genre, même quand j’essaie de me distraire, y’a toujours cette voix qui chuchote que je mérite pas mieux.

Des fois, je me dis que si je pouvais juste… disparaître pour un bout, ça ferait une pause. Mais c’est égoïste, faque je reste là, à essayer de faire semblant que tout est correct. Pis c’est épuisant.
Support: Cela peut être très épuisant de se sentir ainsi, comme si vous étiez en train de porter un poids constant. Il est important de reconnaître que ces pensées et ces sentiments sont difficiles à gérer, et que cela ne signifie pas que vous êtes égoïste de vouloir une pause. Il est possible que vous ayez besoin d'un soutien pour traverser cette période difficile. Avez-vous déjà parlé à quelqu'un de confiance de ce que vous traversez ?
User: J’en ai parlé à ma blonde une fois, mais j’ai minimisé ça en disant que c’était "juste un down". Elle a essayé de m’aider, mais j’ai l’impression de lui voler son énergie, faque j’ai arrêté.

Pis ma famille… oublie ça. Y’a juste des "t’es trop sensible" ou "arrête de dramatiser". Faque là, je me sens encore plus seule avec ça. Comme si personne pouvait vraiment comprendre sans me juger.
Support: Il peut être très dur de se sentir incomprise et jugée par les personnes qui devraient vous soutenir. Cela peut renforcer le sentiment de solitude et de déconnexion. Il est important de savoir que vous n'êtes pas seule dans ces sentiments, et que il existe des personnes qui peuvent vous écouter sans juger. Voulez-vous que je reste ici avec vous un moment, pour simplement écouter et vous offrir un espace de parole ?
User: Merci… c’est weird, mais juste savoir que quelqu’un est là sans vouloir "réparer" ça tout de suite, ça fait du bien.

Faque ouais, si t’es game, j’aimerais ça juste… vider mon sac un peu. Sans pression, sans solutions. Juste exister sans me sentir comme un fardeau pour une fois.
Support: Je suis là pour écouter, sans jugement et sans pression. Vous pouvez prendre tout le temps qu'il vous faut pour partager vos pensées et vos sentiments, sans avoir à craindre de être interrompue ou de devoir trouver des solutions. C'est un espace juste pour vous, pour exister et pour être entendue. Continuez si vous le souhaitez, je suis là pour vous écouter.
User: Faque là, c’est con, mais des fois je me dis que si je pouvais juste… effacer certaines parties de moi, ça irait mieux. Genre, mon corps, mes cicatrices, même ma voix – tout ça me rappelle des affaires que j’essaie d’oublier.

Pis c’est pas que je veux vraiment disparaître, mais des fois, j’ai juste envie de me cacher dans un trou jusqu’à ce que ça fasse moins mal. Comme si mon existence même était une erreur que je peux pas corriger.
Support: Cela peut être très douloureux de se sentir ainsi, comme si des parties de vous-même étaient des sources de douleur et de honte. Il est compréhensible que vous vous sentiez ainsi, comme si vous vouliez vous cacher ou effacer ces parties de vous-même pour échapper à la douleur. Je suis là pour vous écouter et pour vous offrir un espace où vous pouvez exprimer ces sentiments sans jugement. Voulez-vous continuer à explorer ces sentiments et à voir où ils vous mènent ?
User: C’est comme si mon corps était un rappel constant de tout ce que j’ai "foiré" dans ma vie. Des fois, je me surprends à serrer les poings jusqu’à ce que mes ongles laissent des marques, juste pour me sentir un peu plus "réelle".

Pis le pire, c’est que je sais que c’est pas logique, mais j’ai l’impression que si je pouvais juste… arrêter d’exister pour un bout, ça soulagerait tout le monde. Même si c’est juste dans ma tête.
Support: Cela peut être très difficile de se sentir ainsi, comme si votre corps était un symbole de vos échecs et de vos douleurs. Il est compréhensible que vous cherchiez des moyens de vous sentir plus "réelle" ou de vous ancrer dans le présent. Il est important de savoir que ces sentiments ne sont pas une faiblesse, mais plutôt une expression de votre douleur et de votre vulnérabilité. Je suis là pour vous écouter et pour vous offrir un espace de parole, sans jugement ni pression.
User: Faque là, je me dis que si je pouvais juste… dormir pendant des semaines, peut-être que ça irait mieux. Comme si mon cerveau avait besoin d’un reset, mais que chaque fois que j’essaie, je me réveille encore plus épuisée.

Pis c’est pas que je veux vraiment dormir pour toujours, mais des fois, j’ai juste envie que tout s’arrête, même si c’est juste pour un moment. Comme si mon corps et ma tête refusaient de me laisser tranquille.
Support: Cela peut être très épuisant de se sentir ainsi, comme si vous étiez constamment en train de lutter contre vous-même. Il est compréhensible que vous souhaitiez un répit, un moment de paix et de calme. Il est important de reconnaître que ces sentiments sont une expression de votre fatigue et de votre douleur, et qu'ils ne sont pas une faiblesse. Je suis là pour vous écouter et pour vous offrir un espace de parole, sans jugement ni pression, pour que vous puissiez contin
User: Faque là, je me surprends à compter les heures jusqu’à ce que je puisse juste… m’écraser. Comme si la seule chose qui me faisait du bien, c’était l’idée de plus avoir à *faire* quoi que ce soit.

Pis c’est con, mais des fois, je me dis que si je pouvais juste disparaître dans mon lit pour de bon, au moins je ferais plus chier personne. Même si je sais que c’est pas vrai.
